query: What Con Edison commercial customer service class pays the highest demand charge in Manhattan, New York City?
final_answer:
# Con Edison Commercial Demand Charges in Manhattan, NYC

Based on the OpenEI IURDB tariff data for Consolidated Edison Co. of New York (EIA ID: 4226), the commercial service class with the **highest demand charge in Manhattan** is:

## **SC-8 — Multiple Dwellings Redistribution Low Tension Service (Zone I [NYC])**

| Season | Base Rate ($/kW) | Adjustments ($/kW) | **Total Demand Charge ($/kW/month)** |
|--------|-----------------|-------------------|--------------------------------------|
| **Winter** (Oct–May) | $53.85 | $14.69 | **$68.54** |
| **Summer** (Jun–Sep) | $41.56 | $14.69 | **$56.25** |

> **Source:** [OpenEI IURDB — SC-8 Low Tension Zone I NYC](https://apps.openei.org/IURDB/rate/view/67b66529c1ba7352cd0c084f), effective February 1, 2025. Adjustments include Dynamic Load Management, EV Make Ready, Value of Distributed Energy Resources, Market Supply Charge, and Arrears Management.

---

### How It Compares to Other Top NYC Commercial Demand Charges

| Rank | Service Class (NYC) | Peak Season | Total Demand Charge ($/kW/mo) |
|------|---------------------|-------------|-------------------------------|
| 1 | **SC-8 Low Tension Zone I** | Winter | **$68.54** |
| 2 | **SC-8 Low Tension Zone J** | Winter | **$68.54** |
| 3 | SC-12 Space Heating Low Tension Zone I | Winter | $66.77 |
| 4 | SC-12 Space Heating Low Tension Zone J | Winter | $66.77 |
| 5 | SC-8 Low Tension (Delivery Only) | Winter | $55.14 |
| 6 | SC-12 TOD Zone I | Peak TOD tier | $54.30 |
| 7 | SC-9 Low Tension Zone I | Winter | $53.14 |
| 8 | SC-9 Voluntary TOD Zone I | Peak TOD tier | $50.18 |

### Key Takeaways

- **SC-8 (Multiple Dwellings Redistribution Low Tension)** serves commercial customers in multi-tenant buildings with demand requirements between 10 kW and 1,500 kW, at secondary (low) voltage.
- **Zone I** corresponds to Manhattan (roughly south of 96th Street), which is Con Edison's highest-cost delivery zone within NYC.
- The winter demand charge of **$68.54/kW** is the highest of any Con Edison commercial tariff in Manhattan — meaning a customer with just 100 kW of contract demand would pay **$6,854/month** in demand charges alone during winter months.
- The "Delivery with Standard Offer" variants (Zone I and J) include both delivery and commodity (market supply) charges, which is why their adjustments are significantly higher than the delivery-only versions.

- The "Delivery with Standard Offer" variants (Zone I and J) include both delivery and commodity (market supply) charges, which is why their adjustments are significantly higher than the delivery-only versions.
